questions.question: What does a negative score on the Dietary Inflammatory Index (DII) indicate?

options.text: A harmful, pro-inflammatory dietary pattern
A protective, anti-inflammatory dietary pattern
That you are not eating enough food
That the assessment was not completed correctly

questions.explanation: On the DII, negative scores indicate an anti-inflammatory diet (protective) while positive scores indicate a pro-inflammatory diet (harmful). Scores range from about -8.87 to +7.98, with the average American diet scoring +1 to +2 (leaning pro-inflammatory).

questions.question: According to the Mahdavi et al. 2024 study (n=3,501), what was the increase in anxiety scores for every one-unit increase in DII score?

options.text: +1.25 points
+3.02 points
+4.26 points
+7.50 points

questions.explanation: Mahdavi et al. (2024) found that for every one-unit increase in DII score, participants showed +4.26 points in anxiety, along with +3.55 points in perceived stress and +3.02 points in depression on standardized psychological scales. These are meaningful effect sizes that can represent the difference between subclinical symptoms and diagnosable distress.

questions.question: How does the inflammatory cytokine IL-6 contribute to depression?

options.text: It increases serotonin production too quickly, causing mood instability
It directly destroys dopamine receptors in the brain
It diverts tryptophan away from serotonin production toward the pro-inflammatory kynurenine pathway
It prevents the body from absorbing any food nutrients

questions.explanation: IL-6, elevated by high-sugar and high-fat diets, reduces serotonin availability by diverting its precursor (tryptophan) away from serotonin production and toward the pro-inflammatory kynurenine pathway. This represents a double hit — reducing serotonin while simultaneously producing neurotoxic metabolites.

questions.question: Why does a pro-inflammatory diet create a self-reinforcing cycle that is difficult to break?

options.text: Pro-inflammatory foods are addictive and contain chemicals similar to drugs
Elevated inflammatory cytokines increase cravings for the high-sugar, high-fat foods that drive more inflammation
Once you start eating inflammatory foods, your taste buds permanently change
Grocery stores only stock pro-inflammatory foods

questions.explanation: Inflammation itself changes eating behavior. Elevated cytokines increase cravings for high-sugar, high-fat foods — the very foods that drive more inflammation. This creates a feedback loop: pro-inflammatory diet leads to increased cytokines, which leads to brain changes, which leads to cravings for inflammatory foods, which worsens the diet further.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach for shifting your DII score toward anti-inflammatory according to the 80/20 principle?

options.text: Eliminate all pro-inflammatory foods immediately and permanently
Only eat raw fruits and vegetables
Focus on adding anti-inflammatory foods while aiming for 80% healthy choices, leaving 20% for flexibility
Take anti-inflammatory supplements instead of changing your diet

questions.explanation: The 80/20 approach means that when approximately 80% of your dietary choices are anti-inflammatory, the overall inflammatory load drops significantly. This leaves room for flexibility without guilt. Research shows that adding anti-inflammatory foods is as impactful as removing pro-inflammatory ones — shifting the DII even 1-2 points through additions alone can meaningfully reduce stress and anxiety.

title: Anti-Inflammatory Eating and the DII - Quiz
